categories.name: Evidence
categories.guidance: Make sure you have considered all available evidence before making your decision.

items.text: I have considered all the evidence on file, including the applicant's own account
items.guidance: The applicant's PIP2 form is evidence. Do not dismiss it without explanation.

items.text: I have considered evidence from all sources, not just medical reports
items.guidance: Evidence from carers, family members, support workers, and the applicant themselves can all be relevant.

items.text: Where evidence is missing or incomplete, I have recorded why I proceeded without it
items.guidance: If you could not obtain evidence, explain what steps were taken and why the available evidence is sufficient.

items.text: Where evidence conflicts, I have explained which evidence I preferred and why
items.guidance: If the GP report says one thing and the applicant says another, your reasoning must address the conflict.

categories.name: Legal test
categories.guidance: Make sure you have applied the correct legal test for each component.

items.text: I have applied the correct descriptors for each activity
items.guidance: Check you have used the PIP descriptors from Schedule 1 of the 2013 Regulations, not ESA or other benefit descriptors.

items.text: I have considered the required period condition (3 months past, 9 months future)
items.guidance: The applicant must have had the condition for at least 3 months and be likely to continue for at least 9 months.

items.text: I have considered whether the applicant can complete activities reliably, repeatedly, safely, and in a timely manner
items.guidance: Even if someone can do an activity, if they cannot do it reliably, repeatedly, safely, or in a reasonable time, they may still score points.

items.text: I have considered fluctuating conditions and how they affect the applicant on the majority of days
items.guidance: For conditions that vary, consider the impact over a period rather than on a single day.

categories.name: Reasoning and consistency
categories.guidance: Your reasoning must be clear, specific, and consistent with similar decisions.

items.text: My reasoning references specific evidence for each finding
items.guidance: Do not make general statements. Say which document, which page, and what it says.

items.text: I have explained why the evidence does or does not meet each descriptor
items.guidance: A finding of 'criteria not met' needs just as much reasoning as 'criteria met'.

items.text: I have checked similar past decisions for consistency
items.guidance: If your decision differs from similar cases, explain why the facts of this case justify a different outcome.

items.text: My decision would be understandable to the applicant if they read it
items.guidance: Write as if the applicant will read your reasoning. Avoid jargon and explain your logic.

categories.name: Decision notice
categories.guidance: The decision notice must be clear, complete, and written in plain English.

items.text: The decision notice clearly states the outcome
items.guidance: The applicant must be able to understand what has been decided within the first paragraph.

items.text: The decision notice explains the reasoning in plain English
items.guidance: Avoid legal jargon. Use short sentences. Explain what the evidence showed and how you applied the rules.

items.text: The decision notice includes appeal rights and how to appeal
items.guidance: The applicant must be told they can appeal to an independent tribunal and given the deadline.

items.text: I have checked the notice for factual errors (names, dates, amounts)
items.guidance: Check the applicant's name, the decision date, the original decision date, and any award amounts.

categories.name: Evidence
categories.guidance: Make sure you have considered all available evidence before making your decision.

items.text: I have considered all the evidence on file, including the applicant's ESA50 questionnaire
items.guidance: The ESA50 is the applicant's own account of their limitations. It must be considered even if medical evidence is available.

items.text: I have considered evidence of the applicant's mental health as well as physical health
items.guidance: Many ESA cases involve both physical and mental health conditions. Make sure you have assessed both.

items.text: Where evidence is missing, I have recorded why I proceeded without it
items.guidance: If a specialist report was not available, explain what steps were taken to obtain it.

items.text: I have considered the applicant's treatment and whether it affects their capability
items.guidance: Side effects of medication and the impact of treatment itself can affect work capability.

categories.name: Legal test
categories.guidance: Make sure you have applied the correct work capability assessment descriptors.

items.text: I have scored each relevant descriptor and recorded the points
items.guidance: The threshold is 15 points. Record the score for each descriptor, not just the total.

items.text: I have considered the support group descriptors separately from the LCW descriptors
items.guidance: Even if someone scores 15+ points for LCW, you must also consider whether they meet a support group descriptor.

items.text: I have considered Regulation 29 (exceptional circumstances for LCW)
items.guidance: If the applicant does not score 15 points but there would be a substantial risk to their health if found fit for work, Regulation 29 may apply.

items.text: I have considered Regulation 35 (exceptional circumstances for support group)
items.guidance: If the applicant does not meet a Schedule 3 descriptor but there would be a substantial risk if required to undertake work-related activity, Regulation 35 may apply.

categories.name: Reasoning and consistency
categories.guidance: Your reasoning must be clear, specific, and consistent with similar decisions.

items.text: My reasoning references specific evidence for each descriptor scored
items.guidance: For each descriptor where points are awarded or not awarded, cite the evidence.

items.text: I have explained the points score clearly
items.guidance: Show which descriptors scored points, how many, and why.

items.text: I have checked similar past decisions for consistency
items.guidance: If your decision differs from similar cases, explain why.

items.text: My decision would be understandable to the applicant if they read it
items.guidance: Write clearly. Explain what the evidence showed and how you applied the rules.

categories.name: Decision notice
categories.guidance: The decision notice must be clear, complete, and written in plain English.

items.text: The decision notice clearly states the outcome and which group the applicant is placed in
items.guidance: State clearly: fit for work, work-related activity group, or support group.

items.text: The decision notice explains the reasoning in plain English
items.guidance: Avoid jargon. Explain the points score and what it means.

items.text: The decision notice includes appeal rights
items.guidance: The applicant must be told they can appeal and given the deadline.

items.text: I have checked the notice for factual errors
items.guidance: Check names, dates, and the points score.

categories.name: Evidence
categories.guidance: Make sure you have considered all available evidence about the applicant's care needs.

items.text: I have considered all the evidence on file, including the applicant's AA1 form
items.guidance: The AA1 form is the applicant's own account. It must be considered.

items.text: I have considered evidence from professional carers, district nurses, or care agencies
items.guidance: Professional care evidence is particularly important for night-time needs.

items.text: I have considered the frequency and duration of care needs, not just whether care is needed
items.guidance: The legal test requires 'frequent' or 'prolonged' attention. Occasional help may not meet the threshold.

items.text: Where evidence is missing, I have recorded why I proceeded without it
items.guidance: If a care log was not available, explain what steps were taken.

categories.name: Legal test
categories.guidance: Make sure you have applied the correct day and night conditions.

items.text: I have assessed the day condition and the night condition separately
items.guidance: Lower rate requires meeting either condition. Higher rate requires meeting both.

items.text: For the day condition, I have considered both 'frequent attention' and 'continual supervision'
items.guidance: The applicant only needs to meet one of these two tests for the day condition.

items.text: For the night condition, I have considered both 'prolonged or repeated attention' and 'watching over'
items.guidance: The applicant only needs to meet one of these two tests for the night condition.

items.text: I have considered the 6-month qualifying period
items.guidance: The applicant must have needed care for at least 6 months, or be terminally ill.

categories.name: Reasoning and consistency
categories.guidance: Your reasoning must be clear and specific.

items.text: My reasoning references specific evidence for each condition assessed
items.guidance: Cite the care log, GP letter, or other evidence for each finding.

items.text: I have explained why the evidence does or does not meet the day and night conditions
items.guidance: Be specific about frequency, duration, and type of care.

items.text: I have checked similar past decisions for consistency
items.guidance: If your decision differs from similar cases, explain why.

items.text: My decision would be understandable to the applicant if they read it
items.guidance: Write clearly. Explain what the evidence showed.

categories.name: Decision notice
categories.guidance: The decision notice must be clear, complete, and written in plain English.

items.text: The decision notice clearly states the outcome and the rate awarded
items.guidance: State clearly: lower rate, higher rate, or no award.

items.text: The decision notice explains which conditions were met and which were not
items.guidance: Explain the day condition and night condition findings separately.

items.text: The decision notice includes appeal rights
items.guidance: The applicant must be told they can appeal and given the deadline.

items.text: I have checked the notice for factual errors
items.guidance: Check names, dates, and award details.
